intro: Losing a job is scary, especially with a little one counting on you. You are not alone, and asking for help is the smart, strong move — these programs exist for exactly this moment. Start with one call below. You don't have to do it all today.

startHere.title: Start here: call 2-1-1
startHere.body: One free call, any time day or night, connects you to almost everything — rent, utilities, food, child care, and more. A real person helps you figure out what you qualify for. Free, private, no judgment.

sections.title: Keep your home
sections.subtitle: Behind or worried about rent? Ask early — these help most before it's overdue.

resources.name: Polk County rent help
resources.what: One-time help with rent for Polk County families going through a rough patch.
resources.who: Polk County residents with a sudden hardship.
resources.how: Call to start — they'll tell you which papers to bring.
resources.whatToSay: Hi, I'm a Polk County mom who just lost my job. I'm worried about rent this month and I was told you might be able to help.

resources.name: Iowa Legal Aid
resources.what: Free lawyers who help if you're facing eviction or having a problem with a landlord.
resources.how: Call Monday–Friday, 9am–4pm, or apply online anytime.
resources.whatToSay: Hi, I got an eviction notice and I need free legal help. I'm a single mom.

sections.title: Lights, heat & water
sections.subtitle: Help paying the power bill — and stopping a shut-off.

resources.name: IMPACT energy assistance (LIHEAP)
resources.what: Helps pay your heating and electric bills, and can stop your power from being shut off.
resources.who: Polk and Warren county households with low income.
resources.how: Call to apply. If your power is already off after hours, call 515-681-8472.
resources.whatToSay: Hi, I just lost my job and I need help with my energy bill and to keep my power on.

resources.name: Polk County utility help
resources.what: One-time help with a past-due power or water bill.
resources.who: Polk County residents with a temporary hardship.
resources.whatToSay: Hi, I have a past-due power bill and I need a little help catching up.

sections.title: Food for you and your kid
sections.subtitle: Free groceries near you, plus food stamps.

resources.name: DMARC food pantries
resources.what: Free groceries from pantries all over Polk County. You can go once a month, and they deliver too.
resources.how: Call or use the website to find your closest pantry and what to bring.
resources.whatToSay: Hi, I need help with groceries this week. Can you tell me my closest pantry and what to bring?

resources.name: Food stamps (SNAP)
resources.what: Monthly money on a card to buy food. A lot of families qualify — it's worth applying.
resources.how: Apply by phone (about 20 minutes) or online through Iowa HHS.
resources.whatToSay: Hi, I'd like to apply for food assistance. I just lost my job and I have a 6-year-old.

sections.title: Child care help
sections.subtitle: So you can work or interview without worrying.

resources.name: Iowa Child Care Assistance (CCA)
resources.what: Helps pay for child care while you work, look for work, or train.
resources.who: Working or job-seeking parents under the income limit.
resources.how: Apply online through Iowa HHS, or call the free help line below.

resources.name: Free child care help line (CCR&R)
resources.what: Free help finding child care — including before/after school — and applying for help to pay for it.
resources.whatToSay: Hi, I'm a single mom job-hunting and I need help finding child care I can afford for my 6-year-old.

sections.title: Cash & income help
sections.subtitle: Money coming in while you search.

resources.name: Unemployment benefits (IowaWORKS)
resources.what: Weekly payments while you look for work, if you lost your job through no fault of your own.
resources.how: Make a free account at iowaworks.gov and file. File every single week to keep getting paid.

resources.name: Family cash help (FIP)
resources.what: Monthly cash help for families with children and very low income.
resources.how: Apply through Iowa HHS — the same place as food assistance.

sections.title: Health coverage
sections.subtitle: Low-cost or free coverage for you and your child.

resources.name: Hawki — kids' health coverage
resources.what: Low-cost or free health and dental coverage for your child.
resources.whatToSay: Hi, I'd like to sign my child up for Hawki health coverage.

resources.name: Iowa Medicaid — your coverage
resources.what: Free or low-cost health coverage for adults with low income.
resources.how: Apply online through Iowa HHS, or ask 211 to walk you through it.

sections.title: More help for moms
sections.subtitle: Little-known help that makes a real difference.

resources.name: Dress for Success Des Moines
resources.what: A free professional outfit for job interviews, plus career coaching — all free for women.
resources.how: Usually by referral from a nonprofit or job program — ask 211 or a pantry to refer you.
resources.whatToSay: Hi, I have a job interview coming up and I need help with professional clothes.

resources.name: Half-price bus (DART Ride to Thrive)
resources.what: Ride the city bus for about half price if you get food, housing, or unemployment help.
resources.how: Sign up at DART Central Station, 620 Cherry St, Des Moines. Good for 2 years.

resources.name: Diapers & baby supplies
resources.what: Many local pantries also give free diapers and wipes — 211 can point you to the closest one.
resources.whatToSay: Hi, I need help with diapers and a few baby supplies. Where's the closest place?

skillsIntro: These are 100% free — no cost, no debt. The quick ones can strengthen your resume in a weekend; the bigger ones open the door to remote and office jobs that pay more.

skills.name: Free Excel & computer classes
skills.what: Short, free lessons in Excel, Word, email, and basics — the exact skills office and remote jobs ask for.
skills.time: A few hours each · free · no sign-up

skills.name: Free typing practice
skills.what: Practice typing to get faster and more accurate — data-entry and admin jobs love a quick typist.

skills.name: Google Career Certificates
skills.what: Real certificates in IT Support, Data Analytics, or Project Management that lead to remote jobs — and they're free if you ask for aid.
skills.how: On the course page, click 'Financial aid available' and fill out the short form.
skills.time: 3–6 months · a few hours a week

skills.name: Free training through IowaWORKS
skills.what: The state can pay for short job training and help you find work — in person here in Des Moines.
skills.how: Make a free account at iowaworks.gov, or visit 1000 E Grand Ave.

skills.name: Free college tuition (DMACC Last-Dollar)
skills.what: Covers tuition for in-demand short programs at DMACC, like medical office or bookkeeping.
skills.who: Iowa residents in eligible programs who file the FAFSA.

skills.name: Merit America (free, online)
skills.what: A free, part-time online program built for working adults that leads to IT, data, or project jobs you can do from home.

skills.name: Free classes with a library card
skills.what: A free Des Moines Public Library card unlocks online classes and resume help.

safetyNote: Real help is always free. No real program, charity, or job will ever ask you to pay a fee, buy gift cards, or send money to get help or get hired. If anyone asks for that, it's a scam — hang up. When in doubt, call 2-1-1 and ask.
